content.body: I disabled comments on commits for a bunch more WHATWG repositories. I don't think anyone has been relying on that as I would've seen it, but let me know if this creates a problem.
Also, if someone has a connect at GitHub that could turn that into an organization setting: I think we'd love that.
I had completely forgot I’d said I was gonna do that, sorry
I will be a bit late, but do plan to attend WHATNOT

(For `muted`):

  On September 25, 2010 IRC:
  > "[04:18] <Hixie> i don't see any way to have a muted="" content attribute that is reflected by the IDL attribute, since it would mean that if the user had requested a blanket mute of all audio... it would magically sprout a muted="" attribute in the DOM each
  time the document was parsed"

So it sounds like a `defaultValue` sort of reasoning
content.formatted_body:
(For muted):

On September 25, 2010 IRC:

"[04:18] <Hixie> i don't see any way to have a muted="" content attribute that is reflected by the IDL attribute, since it would mean that if the user had requested a blanket mute of all audio... it would magically sprout a muted="" attribute in the DOM each
time the document was parsed"

So it sounds like a defaultValue sort of reasoning

content.body: Right, but that doesn't explain the creation-time magic.
annevk: where did you see that an import map key is a tuple? https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/webappapis.html#module-specifier-map seems to be string->url
content.formatted_body: annevk: where did you see that an import map key is a tuple? https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/webappapis.html#module-specifier-map seems to be string->url

content.body: Noam Rosenthal: I thought we were discussing the module map. Was I confused?
content.formatted_body: Noam Rosenthal: I thought we were discussing the module map. Was I confused?

content.body: Yes I think so. The `<style specifier="...">` thing changes the *import map* which doesn't have a "type" but probably should in some form
content.formatted_body: Yes I think so. The <style specifier="..."> thing changes the import map which doesn't have a "type" but probably should in some form

content.body: I think I did read out loud "A module map is a map keyed by tuples consisting of a URL record and a string." so if I was confused nobody corrected me at the time.
Yes, the module map is keyed by tuples. So that's where the confusion is

Sorry, I still haven't entirely wrapped my head around these `<style>` proposals. I find it weird that'd we have `<style specifier>` but not `<script specifier>` for instance. But yes, I can see how it could be used to clobber the import map. (I'm pretty sure someone said module map though and we also discussed the fetching pipeline of these things, none of which really apply to inline style sheets.)
content.formatted_body: Sorry, I still haven't entirely wrapped my head around these <style> proposals. I find it weird that'd we have <style specifier> but not <script specifier> for instance. But yes, I can see how it could be used to clobber the import map.
